title: RFDE: Resolution-Failure-Directed Extraction for Neuro-Symbolic Reasoning
summary: Generated a 12-page publication-ready LaTeX paper on Resolution-Failure-Directed Extraction (RFDE), a neuro-symbolic architecture that inverts control flow so a backward-chaining SLD proof engine drives on-demand LLM-based fact extraction. The paper includes: Abstract, Introduction (problem statement, contributions), Related Work (eager FOL translation, failure-driven generation, backward chaining, benchmarks), Methods (SLD resolution engine, proof-failure-driven extraction, confidence propagation, hallucination classification), Experiments (evaluation setup, overall metrics, per-dataset accuracy, LLM call efficiency, proof trace auditability), Discussion (structural hallucination prevention, accuracy-hallucination tradeoff, measurement asymmetry, limitations), and Conclusion with References. All 3 available figures are included as figure* environments (21:9 aspect ratio, full-width two-column placement): fig2_v0.jpg (accuracy/hallucination overview bar chart), fig3_v0.jpg (accuracy by dataset), and fig4_v0.jpg (LLM call efficiency and cost). BibTeX references were fetched via Semantic Scholar for 11 citations (6 found via API, remaining 5 written from verified paper text including Kakas 1992 ALP, HBLR/Li2025, ARGOS/Zheng2024). The paper compiled cleanly to a 12-page PDF with all citations resolved. Visual review confirmed all figures render correctly, text is well-formatted, no overlapping elements or cut-off text. The paper targets ACL Knowledge Extraction track as specified.
